transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Kavishka, just quickly, when I googled Glean pricing, both sites came up.
Is there a way I can indicate, like, maybe just in the content OS, which one I chose?
Because I did search, and there is only one assignment for Glean pricing.
So should I maybe make another one?
Yeah, I think just duplicate the assignment, and then maybe, like, add a comment or something, saying, you this is AI and this is .com.
Okay, I'll do that.
Hi, Stevie.
Hey, how's it going?
How was the week after Check That Launching?
Is it crazier or is it more chill?
It's both in different ways.
So I think things have slowed down in the sense of we're not just trying to ship, you know, everything that we can possibly ship.
We're trying to slow down to go faster because, you know, some things weren't thought through from the beginning that ended up, you know, making things difficult.
Like the way we were treating category categories and stuff is this major architectural need versus like, no, this is just a way to group thing information together.
So Daniel's Daniel did some changes that I haven't actually.
Been a week for to be able to look at on the onboarding flow and we'll be making some changes.
then, yeah, so certain things have been just like, okay, let's think through things a little bit more.
But then there's also like more surface area because now we have intercom, we have post hoc analytics and recordings to go through.
And so, yeah, it's a little bit different, but I don't think it's ever going to, maybe a year from now, we'll be like, okay.
Maybe, maybe.
Maybe.
But yeah, it feels good because, yeah, there's different things to focus on.
Speaking of which, one of those things is going to be tackling some of those growth tickets.
And so I kind of wanted to make sure.
I did move some from to do to backlog because everything was in to do.
So it was really hard to like, just kind of, you know, narrow down prioritization.
I kept the ones that you mentioned last week, but I also kept some ones that I wasn't sure about.
So if you could kind of go through what is still in, you know, marked labeled as growth in to do to see to make sure I've got your priorities, right?
I mean, there's still a lot of tickets in there.
And so I might move some out that you're like, yeah, these aren't top priority.
Because I'm just trying to keep things a little bit cleaner for engineering to just be able to execute where I don't have to just, you know, tell them for every little thing, what's the priority.
Yeah, let's do that now.
think just like high level, basically, all of our tickets either fall into like one of three categories, I guess it's either about indexing.
So it's like how everything links together.
I'm going to go
We actually made a little tag for that.
Or it's about, like, the quality of the workflows, which is about, like, cool, these pages, you know, they kind of show up on Google, but they're all the way on page 10.
And the main way to move that is to, you know, make the content better.
And then the last bucket is kind of a little bit more of a mixed bag, but it's like, well, we want a new, you know, content pipeline for a new type of content.
Or, you know, some of the things like this, it's like a quality of life.
You know, I think we're spending a lot of time just, like, deleting and recreating pages that we're rewriting, and it would be nice to have a button.
So everything fits into one of those categories.
And then, yeah, I guess on priority, like, maybe, what is the best way to use this linear board so that kind of, that's communicated, like, as effectively as possible?
Yeah, so, like, I mean.
I you're like top five in to do.
Like right now, there's still too many.
For some reason, I thought I had removed enough to where it was like a little bit more manageable because then it's like if there's that many, then we don't know like the ranking accordingly.
But yeah, so I think just making sure we sink in this meeting to make sure the right things are in to do would be the best.
Everything else just needs to stay in backlog because, you know, we have non-growth tickets, just as many non-growth tickets, you know, in to do.
And so it just becomes so cluttered that we can't really, you know, prioritize very well.
So yeah, I think like the top five would be good.
And then, you know, on a weekly cadence, just making sure the right things are in the in that to do list would be really.

And then, and then within those five ranking them, like, you know, within the ticket, like I, like I said, okay, this is, this is really important to the growth team, this blocks something, and then, you know, I can change the, the priority accordingly, compare, comparatively, respective to the rest of the tickets we have.
Cool.

Um, all right, so that's, that's pretty straightforward, we can do the, um, let's see, okay, I, I think it's some combination of this, like, a lot of the indexing tickets are, like, comparatively much smaller, um, was there any ticket, like, just, I don't it's on the spot, but, like, here, where there was, like, a big question mark in your mind that might block them being worked on, or, like,
I think I got clarity on the ones that I needed to, I just, you know, opened up a discussion like in the comments for a couple of them, just to make sure that, you know, we understood.
think Daniel's got the context.
He said that he was going to start working on these this week, the workflow, I think, once.

Um, the answers, yeah, I think I got clarity where I needed to on the, on like where to put some of that information because right now the, that newer related brands is, um, there's kind of a lot there.
You know, um, and so, yeah, just trying to figure.
Figure out, like, how to best organize that information to where it's grokkable.
Like, you don't want just, like, this massive amount of content.
So I'm curious about, I think, yeah, think Savio would have the best context and then also just, like, you know, good design sense to be able to do that.
If you guys think that we need Ren on, you know, making sure, like, that newer information flows well, then we can pull him in.
But, yeah, if you guys have any strong opinions about how the page is and the sidebar, you know, nav should be designed, let me know.
That was, like, kind of the only.
That's only concern I had for adding content to brand pages.
Should we think about there being different lanes for work?
For example, if I just had five internal linking tickets, it's a little different than half those tickets are for internal linking, half those tickets are for workflow improvements.

Like are those different people where it helps to have like one of each and then maybe there's even another group like Renan, George Mayne, for example, I see them more as like, you know, things that require a bit more product thinking or design.
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
I mean, so the, I mean, they're not, they were just helping out, you know, for a short bit there.
But if they have bandwidth, you know, certainly.
The Check That team, the Check That team, engineering team is very experienced.
And incredibly, you know, like very senior, very can run with things.
But when it comes to like the shaping, the design side, you know, making sure it, you know, it makes sense within the page, I think that help there would be good.
So any tickets that are maybe less on the technical, more front end, if we can pull someone that has a lot of design sense, I think that would be beneficial to us.

Okay, so the kind of the lanes are good, because I remember when we did this, like initially the ask was just cool, Ren, could you kind of brainstorm and think through how we would surface more data on these pages?
And then that, they kind of just built in, I was like, oh, cool, that's, that's awesome.
So that's like, maybe less kind of dealing with, like all the data and all the backend stuff.
And it's more just the packaging of
And shifting some things around, that's like one area.
There's like the kind of more serious, like, hey, if we wanted a thing to like categorize brands differently or something, that would just be like a totally different kind of engineering ask.
And then the last bucket is more workflow asks, like, hey, our workflow for alternatives pages could be better in this or that way.
Like, it seems like those are all different people.
I would say that the workflows will, I mean, eventually the whole engineering team will need to be comfortable with working on them because we use workflows internally, right?
Like, so that's just, it's not just a content thing.
But for the content ones right now, yeah, it's just kind of whoever has the extra time because we were just hyper-focused on launch.
So I wouldn't say, I would say that those, you know, blend in with the other deeper engineering.
But, yeah, for the content, they're lighter weight workflows than, you know, say the probing ones, right?
Oh, okay.
I see.
Okay.
That's helpful.

I think I just want to make sure we do a good job, like, kind of keeping that queue always filled, like, not just having a bunch of tickets just for design, then we're leaving other available resources.
Kind of, you know, like, not underutilized, for lack of a better word, because there's just so much.
Yeah, there's definitely no, I don't think anyone's underutilized.
Yeah, I know.
Or just how could we pull, you know, as much.
yeah, yeah.
As I said, like, for more of, like, the layout, the design, I don't, you know, like, we do only have maybe one person that is, want.,兄.
Thanks
Has that, you know, sense versus, so if we can pull someone in that's not working on anything critical and stuff on, like, the content OS stuff that, like, Ren or, that would be great.
But for little stuff like this, I mean, I don't think, like, we need, you know, like, someone, it's, could you actually go back real quick to that related section, though, on the brand page?

Because I did kind of want it, like, if you scroll down, yeah, all the way, yeah, so the above that, so that's what I was kind of trying to, you know, I got clarity on, you know, a little bit of clarity, but yeah.
So, like, a lot of the related information that some of those tickets are specifically, like, for cross-linking and stuff.
That might need a little design love, because it already is kind of a lot that's in there right now.
Oh, I see.
Just like this.
adding to it is going to be a little bit gnarly looking, maybe.
Okay.
That's a good call.
Oh, related categories.
think that one should be in to do, right?
Wasn't that?
Or, I can't remember.
Was that one of the cross-linking ones?
Because there were, like, there were a few, and so it would be easy to do, like, the ones that are very similar to each other.
Yeah, there's quite a lot of interlinks.
Because those can be, like, done in the same PR, probably.
If it's basically doing the same thing, just for different...
Yeah, it's kind of these two.
All right, is there anything else worth calling out, Vishka and Saskia, like tickets?
Do you think this rough cluster of tickets is the most important?
Looks good to me.
Yeah, and if I'm being honest, the one about regenerating articles, now that I think about it, probably not super urgent.

Like, we'll only really need to regenerate articles if the work, like, the underlying workflow is updated and fixed, So this will probably have to come, like, I could do it manually until we have the workflows done.
So we can deprioritize that.
That's pretty cool.
Yeah, I think that's it.
And within those five...
Like, you've got the urgent on the workflows or whatever, but what about the other ones?
Are they all median, like, median priorities?
You don't have to change the prioritization, but is that about right, or does any, like, two, one or two stand out?
I think it really is, like, it really is these two.
Well, those are already assigned to Daniel, right?
Like, these, the other ones are assigned, except for 512 is another one that's assigned.
Yeah, so minus those, I think these three basically are all interlinking.

I would put them lower priority than the workflow, but, like, as a whole, like, we have a lot of indexing problems, so it's still, yeah, like, I would put that as, like, a higher or medium versus urgent.
Okay, yeah, because someone else can work on those if Daniel's going to tackle the workflows.
Yeah.
That's it on our end.
Yeah, so let me know how we can help, and then we'll keep an eye on these tickets.
I'm very bad at checking linear sometimes, but if there is, yeah, just flags and comments, and we'll reply if there's any outstanding questions.
Yeah, we should be able to get to those pretty quickly.
I mean, they don't seem like a big engineering lift, so that's just a matter of, like, working on finishing up some other priorities that we've got going on.
So it'd be more like probably end of the week, towards the end of the week.
Sounds good.
I know all the product stuff is, like, important.
And spending a bunch of money driving more sign-ups, but if they hit a wall, it kind of sucks.
Yeah.
Yeah, yeah.
So we're working on kind of helping them get to that aha moment a lot faster or at all for them after.
You know, if we're doing some sort of like road mapping session, Marcel mentioned it in the office last week.
So we're going to do, so we did update some meetings because, you know, that was always the intention post-launch.
So we're going to, engineering, we're going to get rid of the daily stand-up and go back to our like engineering focused weekly stand-up, or not stand-up, but kind of discussion session.
But a lot of the discussion is going to happen asynchronously in Slack.
And Fridays, it's going to be Daniel, Marcel, and I, and if you're not invited, Daniel said that, know, you can join when.

You have time or bandwidth, but that's going to be kind of a messier strategy meeting that basically I'm going to do a lot of just kind of translating what, you know, their thoughts are and discussions are and trying to guide that as much as I can, but translate that to, you know, what engineering needs to work on for the following week kind of thing.
That's good.
I'm to talk to Marcel later.
I'll just ask him if he wants me there.
If not, I'll focus on growing and selling.
It's on our side.
Appreciate it.
Jason, I just have a question for you before you go.
Yes.
All right.
Stevie, if you want to go, you're...
Yeah, feel free to drop.
Well, thanks guys for letting me take...
Kind of like take that over a little bit, but I just thought it'd be, I'm trying to kind of clean stuff up so we can narrow our focus a little bit tighter.
Oh, sure.
Thank you so much.
Take care.
Bye.
Bye.
Very quick one.
I did run this by Kavishka, but I just wanted to run by you as well.
Let me just find the tab and share my screen.
Okay.
Share screen.
Oh, turn my video.
Where is the share screen button?
See it?
Oh, my goodness.
Just kill me.
Okay.
It's been a long day.
Can you guys see this phone?
Yep.
Okay.
So, basically, I wanted to ask you if it's worth filing it.
Ticket about the headings.
So I know we don't normally, so first of all, the H3 is very similar to the bolded text.
And this becomes an issue when you want an H4.
So I know we're not really like supposed to use H4s, but a lot of the articles that generate are so detailed that like you have an H3 and then there are headings underneath that.
And currently the AI kind of just bolds it and you end up with these really long, weird paragraphs or like bullet lists that aren't bullet lists.
And it's really weird.
So I've been using bolder text as an H4, but that is really similar to the H3.
So my request would be, can we make the H2 a bit bigger, the H3 a bit bigger, and then add an H4 and just have that like distinction between the headings?
Because right now it's really hard to skim read.
That sounds fun to me, I guess.
Yeah, just feel like you should avoid them if possible, but, you know, I'm thinking through, like, the alternatives articles, and it's just, like, you have multiple layers, so.

Yeah, it's mainly for the, I've noticed it's mainly for the answer pages, where it really dives into detail, and, yeah, I just, it's really not skimmable at the moment, and with H4s, just a few of them would really help, but mainly the problem is the H3s being so small.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Um, one, one tactical thing for tickets, it seems like the engineering team wants to use the, like, low, medium, high for their, um.
Yes, it's, it's, it's, like, didn't want us to do that.
Maybe, I wonder what we could do, like, I guess we could do, like, uh, maybe for all these tickets, um, I guess we have it here, so on, Yeah.
Tickets with this, I guess that'll take place, but then tickets without, uh,

think I could.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Align on growth ticket priorities and refine the engineering workflow.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=171.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Prioritization Process:** To improve engineering focus, the "To Do" column will be limited to the top 5 growth tickets. The growth team will rank these tickets internally, as the engineering team uses the `low/medium/high` priority field for their own system.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=284.0)
  - [**Top 5 Tickets:** The immediate focus is on two workflow tickets (assigned to Daniel) and three interlinking tickets. The interlinking tickets are a higher-medium priority, as they address critical indexing issues.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=958.0)
  - [**Design Support Needed:** The "Related brands" section on brand pages is already dense; adding more cross-links requires design input to maintain readability. The team will explore pulling in design-focused resources like Renan.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=799.0)
  - [**New Ticket Process:** All new tickets must be filed in the "Triage" column. This ensures they are reviewed and formally prioritized by the engineering team before being moved to the backlog or a work column.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1389.0)

## Topics

### Engineering Workflow & Prioritization

  - [**Problem:** The "To Do" column is cluttered, making it difficult for engineering to identify and execute on top priorities.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=171.0)
  - [**Solution:** Limit the "To Do" column to the top 5 growth tickets.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=284.0)
      - [The growth team will rank these 5 tickets internally.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)
      - [The engineering team will use the `low/medium/high` priority field for their own system.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1349.0)
  - [**New Ticket Flow:** All new tickets must be filed in "Triage" for formal review and prioritization by the engineering team.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1389.0)

### Top 5 Growth Tickets

  - [**Urgent Priority (Assigned to Daniel):**](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=958.0)
      - [Two workflow tickets.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=958.0)
  - [**Higher-Medium Priority (Unassigned):**](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=958.0)
      - [Three interlinking tickets.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=958.0)
      - [**Rationale:** These tickets address critical indexing issues that are currently blocking user discovery.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)
      - [**Note:** These tickets are low-lift and can be bundled into a single PR.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=878.0)
  - [**Deprioritized:**](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=925.0)
      - [The ticket for regenerating articles.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=925.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This is not urgent and can be handled manually until the underlying workflows are updated.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=925.0)

### Design & Layout Needs

  - [**Problem:** The "Related brands" section on brand pages is already dense. Adding more cross-links without design input will make the page unreadable.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=799.0)
  - [**Solution:** Pull in design-focused resources (e.g., Renan) to shape the layout and ensure a good user experience.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=799.0)

### New Ticket: Heading Structure

  - [**Problem:** The current heading structure hinders skimmability, especially on detailed "answer" pages.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1255.0)
      - [`H3` is too similar to bolded text.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1260.0)
      - [No `H4` style exists, forcing content creators to use bolded text as a workaround, which further reduces clarity.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1272.0)
  - [**Proposed Solution:**](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1255.0)
      - [Increase the font size of `H2` and `H3`.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1255.0)
      - [Introduce a distinct `H4` style.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1255.0)
  - [**Action:** Saskia will create a ticket for this in the "Triage" column.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1389.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Saskia:**](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=24.0)
      - [Create a ticket for the new heading structure (`H2`, `H3`, `H4`) and place it in the "Triage" column.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1255.0)
      - [Duplicate the Glean pricing assignment in the content OS to cover both the `.ai` and `.com` sites.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=24.0)
  - [**Jason:**](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1091.0)
      - [Confirm with Marcel if he should join the new Friday strategy meeting.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1091.0)
  - [**Stevie:**](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=171.0)
      - [Limit the "To Do" column to the top 5 growth tickets.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=284.0)
      - [Assign the three interlinking tickets to an engineer.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=995.0)
  - [**Growth Team:**](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)
      - [Rank the top 5 tickets internally.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)
      - [Use the "Triage" column for all new tickets.](https://fathom.video/share/B7DHYH98qxGxxfnuZqRsaq9hM4tqACoQ?tab=summary&timestamp=1389.0)

action_items.description: Duplicate Glean pricing assignment in Content OS; add comment noting AI vs .com source
Deprioritize regenerate articles ticket in Linear
Ask Marcel re: Fri strategy session invite
File Linear ticket re: heading styles (H2/H3/H4); set low priority; place in Triage
